title: Free the States Memo to OK Senators Regarding SB1728
author: Russell Hunter
orientation: An open memo from Free the States urging Oklahoma senators to oppose SB1728, arguing the bill is a deceptive substitute for SB13 that fails every measure of true abolition and props up the regulatory regime keeping abortion legal.
audiences: Oklahoma state senator weighing the vote
Pro-life Christian voter being asked to back SB1728
Pastor or church leader assessing legislative compromise
Skeptical observer of the abolitionist movement

questions.question: Why does Free the States call SB1728 unjust rather than merely insufficient?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state senator weighing the vote
questions.answer: Because a legislator's duty is to write just laws, and a bill that responds to ongoing mass killing only with optional civil suits over paperwork failures fails to deliver any actual justice to the preborn victims. Allowing parents or grandparents to sue an abortionist for skipping a regulation does nothing to declare the killing itself a crime, so the law continues to authorize the very thing it pretends to address.
questions.quote: Senate Bill 1728 is a woefully inadequate response to the mass murder that is taking place every day under the covering of Oklahoma State law. Your duty as a legislator is to write just laws. **SB1728 is unjust.**

questions.question: What concrete remedy does SB1728 actually provide, and why is it inadequate?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian voter being asked to back SB1728
questions.answer: It only opens the door for post-abortive parents or the grandparents of the killed child to file a civil suit when an abortionist neglects or fails to document compliance with abortion regulations. That is a private lawsuit triggered by a paperwork lapse, not a criminal prohibition on the killing itself, so it leaves the practice fully legal.
questions.quote: Allowing for a civil suit by the post-abortive parents or by the grandparents of the murdered victim if the abortionist fails to adhere to or fails to document his or her adherence to various abortion regulations does nothing to establish justice for preborn human beings.

questions.question: By what standard does Free the States measure abolition bills, and how does SB1728 score?
questions.answer: Free the States measures bills against its 5 Components of an abolition bill, and SB1728 fails on all five: it does not outlaw abortion from conception, allows every exception, does not criminalize abortion in any way, does not stand up to the courts, and does not repeal or supersede existing abortion regulations.
questions.quote: **It fails on all of the 5 Components of an abolition bill.** (See [https://freethestates.org/5-components-of-an-abolition-bill/](https://freethestates.org/5-components-of-an-abolition-bill/index.html) for a full explanation of the 5 Components).

questions.question: Why is being 'another abortion regulation' presented as a strike against SB1728 rather than a feature?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader assessing legislative compromise
questions.answer: Because regulations operate within a framework that presumes abortion is legal; piling on another regulatory layer entrenches that framework and creates one more law that would later have to be repealed before abortion could be abolished. SB1728 therefore moves the goal further away, not closer.
questions.quote: **SB1728 does not repeal or supersede abortion regulations. Further, it is another abortion regulation that would need to be repealed for abortion to be abolished.**

questions.question: What broader political role does Free the States accuse SB1728, HB1182, and SB1859 of playing?
questions.audience: Skeptical observer of the abolitionist movement
questions.answer: It accuses these three bills of being deliberate substitutes for SB13 — face-saving legislation introduced so the Senate can claim pro-life credentials with Christian voters while killing the only bill that would actually establish justice for preborn children.
questions.quote: Further, SB1728 should be opposed because it, along with HB1182 and SB1859, are being used as substitutes for doing your duty as a legislator to write and support just laws. Senate Bill 13 is the bill which would establish justice, and these compromise bills are substitutes to try to save face with Christian voters as this Senate kills the only bill that would establish justice.

questions.question: Why does the memo argue the political cover provided by SB1728 will not work?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state senator weighing the vote
questions.answer: Because organized constituencies — the Oklahoma Baptists, Oklahoma Free Will Baptists, and the Oklahoma Republican Party — already know which bill abolishes abortion and which ones do not. Voting for SB1728 instead of SB13 will be recognized as a dodge rather than accepted as pro-life action.
questions.quote: The people are not going to be fooled. They know which bills abolish abortion and which don’t. The Oklahoma Baptists, Oklahoma Free Will Baptists, and Oklahoma Republican Party are all looking for the bill that would abolish abortion and they know which one it is.

questions.question: On what theological and constitutional ground does the memo dismiss Roe v. Wade?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader assessing legislative compromise
questions.answer: On the ground that any civil command to permit mass murder must be refused; because Roe both violates God's law and the plain meaning of the U.S. Constitution, it is null by its very nature, and no court opinion legalizing murder can ever carry legitimate authority.
questions.quote: When any civil authority commands you to allow mass murder you’re supposed to say ‘no.’ In violating God’s law and the clear meaning of the U.S. Constitution, Roe is null by its nature. No legal opinion that murder should be legal can ever be legitimate

questions.question: How does the memo answer senators who blame Senate President Greg Treat for blocking SB13?
questions.answer: By telling them the excuse is empty: if every senator who privately claims to support SB13 actually got behind it, Treat would have no power to stop the bill, because no senator is king of the chamber.
questions.quote: If everyone who told us that they want to support SB13 but can’t because of Greg Treat got behind SB13, there’s nothing Greg Treat would be able to do. There is no king of the Senate.

questions.question: What specific actions does Free the States demand at the close of the memo?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state senator weighing the vote
questions.answer: Four imperatives: stop compromising, vote against SB1728, refuse to bow to Roe, refuse to bow to Senate President Greg Treat, and use whatever procedural means are available to bring SB13 to the floor for a vote.
questions.quote: **Stop compromising. Oppose SB1728. Stand up to Roe. Stand up to Treat. Support SB13 being brought to the floor.**

related.title: Senate Bill 1728 Fully Affirms Legal Abortion
related.reason: James Silberman's longer follow-up dissecting how SB1728 actually reinforces the legality of abortion — the deeper case behind this memo's bullet points.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13"?
related.reason: Spells out the strategic rationale for refusing to back substitute bills like SB1728 and holding the line on SB13 — the affirmative case this memo presupposes.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Directly answers Senate President Greg Treat's public framing of SB13, the same Treat the memo tells senators to stand up to.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Background on the senator the memo names as the central obstacle to SB13 reaching the floor.

related.title: Motion to Bring SB13 to Senate Floor Coming Tomorrow! Email Your Senator!
related.reason: The activation step that follows this memo — the actual procedural motion the memo is asking senators to support.

related.title: Remember Bullard's Claim That "thousands of unborn babies will be saved every year" by SB1728
related.reason: Targets the specific SB1728 author named by tag in this memo and rebuts his marketing claims for the bill.

related.title: Nullification
related.reason: Expands the doctrine the memo invokes when it tells senators that Roe is null and must be defied — the theological and constitutional argument behind the closing demands.
